steps.title: Find the angle at C in triangle ABC
steps.content:
In triangle ABC, the angle at A is 20 degrees and the angle at B is $x^{\circ} = 100^{\circ}$. Using the triangle angle sum:
$$20^{\circ} + 100^{\circ} + \angle ACB = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle ACB = 60^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Use vertically opposite angles at the intersection point
steps.content:
Lines AD and BE intersect at C, forming two pairs of vertically opposite angles. Since angles ACB and DCE are opposite each other:
$$\angle DCE = \angle ACB = 60^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Find y using the angle sum in triangle CDE
steps.content:
In triangle CDE, we now know the angle at C is 60 degrees and the angle at E is 40 degrees. Using the triangle angle sum:
$$60^{\circ} + 40^{\circ} + y = 180^{\circ}$$
$$y = 80^{\circ}$$
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
